summary: I was born and raised in Key West, Florida, and it holds a special place in my heart. Paul and I lived there for many years before he was transferred to Fort Lauderdale and later Miami. We still have family and friends there, and we often visit because most of our folks live in Key West. I love gardening and used to keep a lovely noisy garden there with Murdock. Someday, we hope to own a home there again if the railroad ever gets built back and Paul can be transferred.

Good Housekeeping is one of the magazines I keep around the house. I often find it on the small table beside the overstuffed chair in the living room. It’s part of the everyday life here, alongside other publications like Unlearn, a photography magazine. I enjoy having these magazines handy while I listen to the radio or relax in the living room.

Paul has worked for the Florida East Coast Railway for the past 14 years as a car repairer. He started in Key West and was later transferred to the shops in Fort Lauderdale and then Miami. His job requires him to be ready at any hour in case of an accident, and he rarely takes time off since he is paid semi-monthly without paid vacation. Despite the hard work, it provides steady income and benefits like railroad passes, though we mostly travel by car.

We lived in Fort Lauderdale for a while after Paul was transferred there from Key West. At first, I didn’t like it much, but over time we made many friends. We rented a house near the railroad shops so Paul’s commute was easy and the children could get to school without much trouble. Still, I preferred Key West and eventually we moved again to Miami.

Miami is where we live now after Paul’s transfer from Fort Lauderdale. I didn’t care much for Miami at first, especially since the rent is higher here compared to Key West. We rented a furnished house for a year near Paul’s work, which is convenient for the family. I’m slowly getting used to the city, but I still dream of going back to Key West someday.

When we moved from Key West to Fort Lauderdale, we bought a Chevrolet because it was hard to get around without a car there. Paul usually takes the car in the morning, but if I want it during the day, I keep it and call for him at four o’clock. It’s been very useful for visiting around town and running errands. Before that, in Key West, we managed without a car just fine.
cues: bought after moving to Fort Lauderdale

summary: Celia, our youngest, had whooping cough recently. Thankfully, our family has good health overall, so we don’t spend much on doctors or medicine. It was a worrying time, but she recovered well. We try to take good care of the children’s health, especially since Paul works hard and we want to keep the family strong.
